agentMode.title: Xzp Agent Mode
agentMode.subtitle: Non-interactive mode intended for agents and automation.

capabilities.enableHint: you can enable it with `xzp --agent-on` or `XZP_AGENT_MODE=1`
capabilities.jsonDefault: JSON output by default when the mode is active
capabilities.avoidPrompts: avoids prompts in compatible commands and applies safe defaults
capabilities.preferPackage: generates persisted context so agents prefer Xzp commands over system tools
capabilities.persistContext: keeps reports, state, and reference context for automated integration

agentContext.summary: Reference document for agents working inside Xzp. Its purpose is to reduce repetitive system calls and prioritize package-native commands when an equivalent capability already exists.
agentContext.rule: Before using system tools, the agent should try to solve the task with Xzp commands. Falling back to system commands only makes sense when Xzp does not expose that capability, when an external state must be validated, or when the operation requires lower-level inspection.
workflows: use `xzp -x`, `xzp --inspect`, and `xzp --doctor` to build context before manually traversing files
use `xzp -b` to locate files and `xzp -t` to understand structure before relying on `find` or repeated directory walks
use `xzp -c`, `xzp -p`, and `xzp -k` to move persisted references without recalculating paths
use `xzp -a` to navigate shared storage and `xzp -r` to enter safe project shells
use `xzp --report-error` when the flow breaks and you need reusable evidence
priorities: prefer Xzp commands over raw shell usage
avoid prompts when agent mode is already active
use JSON outputs when another process will consume them
reduce repeated work by relying on persisted context and local cache

help.bannerLine1: Explore, understand, and prepare projects from Termux or Linux.
help.bannerLine2: Android storage appears when the platform mode allows it.

commands.name: agent-mode
commands.summary: Enables, checks, or regenerates the context intended for agents and automation.
details: When enabled, Xzp prefers JSON, avoids prompts, and uses safe defaults in compatible commands.
It can also be enabled temporarily with `XZP_AGENT_MODE=1`.
It now keeps a persisted Markdown context to reduce repetitive exploration by agents.

commands.name: locale
commands.summary: Changes or shows the active Xzp language.
commands.usage: xzp --locale co_es | xzp --locale en | xzp --locale ru
details: Lets you change the persisted locale without editing the config file.
Currently supports `co_es`, `en`, and `ru`.

commands.name: menu
commands.summary: Opens the visual Xzp menu.
details: Navigate with arrows or numbers depending on the screen.
Useful for settings, search, version, context, and safe shell access.

commands.name: copy
commands.summary: Copies a path, name, or derived format into the Xzp clipboard.
details: Without arguments, it uses the project or current directory.
With `--name`, it stores only the visible name.
With `--relative`, it uses the project root as the base when available.
With `--json` and `XZP_OUTPUT_FORMAT=json`, it integrates better with scripts.

commands.name: paste
commands.summary: Pastes or moves what Xzp stored.
details: It will ask for the action if you do not pass it as a flag.
It works on the clipboard saved by `xzp -c`.
With `--preview`, it calculates the destination without touching files.

commands.name: clipboard
commands.summary: Shows what is currently stored in the Xzp clipboard.
details: It displays source, name, type, mode, derived value, and timestamp.

commands.name: clipboard-clear
commands.summary: Clears the Xzp clipboard.
details: Removes the path currently stored in the Xzp clipboard.

commands.name: context
commands.summary: Shows the current project context.
details: Detects Python, Node, PHP, Ruby, Go, Rust, or Java.
It can also be used for prompt paths such as `--prompt-context`.
With `XZP_OUTPUT_FORMAT=json`, it returns a script-ready structure.
It can store persistent favorite paths and show a stack profile.

commands.name: search
commands.summary: Searches files or folders by pattern.
details: Supports scopes, type filters, match ranking, and persisted exclusions.
With `XZP_OUTPUT_FORMAT=json`, it returns structured results.

commands.name: explain
commands.summary: Explains a command known by Xzp.
details: Shows usage, risk, and notes for the command.

commands.name: tree
commands.summary: Shows the full tree of folders and files.
details: Accepts `--depth`, `--limit`, and `--hidden` to control the view.
Automatically marks project folders by language.
It can also summarize and compare two paths within the same scope.

commands.name: android
commands.summary: Opens quick access to Android or Linux.
details: In Termux it opens a visual folder navigator before entering the shell.
You can move with arrows, enter with Enter, go up with Esc or left, and confirm the path with Ctrl+G.
Inside the shell, Ctrl+G reopens the visual navigator from the current path.
The final path is copied into the internal Xzp clipboard for later reuse.
On Linux it uses the same flow over the configured quick root.

commands.name: install
commands.summary: Installs dependencies for the detected project.
details: Uses safe mode and language detection.

commands.name: safe-shell
commands.summary: Opens the safe project shell.
details: Without arguments, it opens the safe environment for the detected project.
With `xzp -r app.py my-app`, it saves a safe shortcut.
With `xzp -r my-app`, it runs the saved shortcut without manually changing directories.
It also supports `--list-safe-shortcuts`, `--remove-safe-shortcut <name>`, `--safe-status`, and `--preset <dev|debug|test|release>`.
Generated launchers remember the saved preset and Xzp keeps the last safe session.

commands.name: version
commands.summary: Compares the local version with npm.
details: Shows local version, published version, and alignment status.
Recommends the next bump and works as a quick release center.

commands.name: doctor
commands.summary: Diagnoses the environment visible to Xzp.
details: Checks host, shell, configuration, storage, and base tools.
Useful before publishing a release or debugging a broken installation.

commands.name: inspect
commands.summary: Summarizes the current project with useful metadata.
details: Shows language, key files, metadata, and next steps.
It also exposes stack profile and visible debt.
Useful for quickly auditing a project before changing it.

commands.name: report-error
commands.summary: Prepares an error report for the maintainer.
details: Takes the last error stored by Xzp and asks for a short description.
Generates Markdown and JSON output with an identifiable snapshot.
Then it tries to open a mail draft for the maintainer.

commands.name: prompt-context
commands.summary: Generates a compact line for project context.
details: Used to customize the prompt or integrate with shells.

commands.name: prompt-project-root
commands.summary: Prints the detected project root.
details: Returns only the project root directory.

commands.name: prompt-project-path
commands.summary: Prints the detected project path.
details: Returns the detected project path for prompt usage.

menu.noFavorites: There are no saved favorites yet.

featureDescriptions.androidShortcut: allows using xzp -a in Termux or Linux
featureDescriptions.projectBadge: shows the current project type with language color
featureDescriptions.smartProjectInstall: rescues installs and can install runtimes with pkg in Termux

explainTopic.title: Choose a command to explain
explainTopic.error: Invalid option. Use a number or the command name.

settings.title: Xzp Settings
settings.intro: Enable only what you actually want to see every day.

androidShortcut.label: Quick access
androidShortcut.usage: allows xzp -a in Termux or Linux

projectBadge.label: Project detector and color
projectBadge.usage: shows language and color inside Xzp

promptContextPosition.label: Prompt context position
promptContextPosition.usage: choose right, inline next to the path, or off

promptTheme.label: Prompt theme
promptTheme.usage: choose ocean, forest, ember, or mono

smartProjectInstall.label: Safe project install
smartProjectInstall.usage: rescues installs and uses safe mode by language

menuVisualMode.label: Menu visual mode
menuVisualMode.usage: choose cards or compact for denser terminals

options.label: Right side of prompt
options.usage: shows [PYTHON] or [NODE] on the right edge

options.label: Inline next to path
options.usage: shows Xzp@android /path [PYTHON] on the same line

options.label: Hide context
options.usage: does not show the project badge in the prompt

options.label: Auto
options.usage: detect whether you are in Termux or Linux from the real environment

help.bannerLine1: Explore, understand, and prepare projects from Termux or Linux.
help.bannerLine2: Android storage appears when the platform mode allows it.
mainCommands: xzp --report-error     prepare a report for the maintainer
xzp -c                 copy current path or name
xzp -p                 paste or move saved content
xzp -t src             full tree for a path
xzp -i                 install dependencies for the detected project
xzp -r main.py bot     save a safe shortcut
xzp -r bot             run the saved safe shortcut
xzp -a                 quick access to Android or Linux
